short_description: 8-channel radiometer, 5 channels non-scanning, 3 scanning. Of the non-scanning, one is for solar irradiance (0.2-50 μm), two for earth radiation in SW (0.2-5.0 μm) over wide-FOV and medium-FOV respectively, two for total earth radiation (0.2-50 μm) over wide-FOV and medium-FOV respectively.  The three scanning channels cover the ranges 0.2-5.0 μm, 0.2-50 μm and 5.0-50 μm respectively
background: Simplified from the ERB peviously flown on Nimbus-6 and Nimbus-7.  Implementing the Earth Radiation Budget Experiment in cooperation with NOAA-9 and NOAA-10

short_description: 8-channel radiometer, 5 channels non-scanning, 3 scanning. short_description: Improved SAFIRE sensor: 3 thermal bands, 200 m, 1.9-day revisit for global wildfire monitoring

short_description: Next-generation SAFIRE nanosatellite with TIR + optical camera; 30-min revisit target
notes: Includes optical imaging instrument alongside TIR. ESA InCubed funded.

short_description: Four-port double pendulum Fourier-Transform interferometer, to cover the range 100-1600 cm-1 (6.25-100 μm) with a spectral resolution of 0.5 cm-1 (unapodised).  An embedded TIR imager centred at 10.5 μm with a bandwidth of 1.5 μm looking at the same scene as the interferometer acquires co-located images during the dwell time for detection of scene inhomogeneities. NedT of sounder 0.1K above 300 cm-1 and 0.2K for 200-300 cm-1.
background: New development

short_description: Spectral scanning by alternating filters placed on the border of a wheel. Two bands, 3.2-6.4 μm and 1.2-2.4 μm; spectral resolution 0.6-1.2 %

short_description: Fourier transform interferometer with large detector arrays for simultaneous sounding of more pixels. The IRS is based on an imaging Fourier-interferometer with a hyperspectral resolution of 0.604cm-1 wave-number, taking measurements in two bands: MWIR: 1600 to 2250cm-1 (4.44–6.25µm) and LWIR: 680 to 1210cm-1 (8.26–14.70µm).Monitoring and calibration informationhere
background: First operational hyperspectral infrared sounder in GEO at 0° longitude; based on IASI heritage.
scanning_technique: The Earth disc is covered in 4 latitude belts (Local Area Coverage, LAC) which covered in an interleaved, 6-hourly pattern of step-and-stare “dwells” of the instrument. The northermost belt that includes Europe (LAC4), is imaged at 30-min intervals for the whole 6-hour period (12 images). The following belt to the South (LAC3) is covered in two sequences of three images in 30-minute intervals, separated by 2 hours (total of 6 images). The other two belts in the Southern Hemisphere (LAC2, LAC1) are each covered by a sequence of 3 images in 30-minute intervals, separated by 5 hours (each a total of 3 images). Each dwell has a duration of about 10s and consist of 160x160 pixels (soundings), i.e 25600 spectra in 1960 spectral channels per dwell. It takes 15 minutes to complete the scan of a belt (LAC). More details and scanning animation at https://www.eumetsat.int/mtg-infrared-sounder
primary_objectives: Height of the top of PBL

background: Derivative of the 25-channel MERSI-2 flown on FY-3D, replacing the 19 SW channel by a Low-light VIS channel, for use in a dawn-dusk orbit

short_description: 8-channel VIS/IR radiometer [see details in table below]Calibration monitoringhere
background: Simplified version of MERSI for the Rainfall Mission

full_name: Meteorological Imager (Previous name: VII - Visible and Infrared Imager )
short_description: 20 VIR/IR channels [see detailed characteristics below]Monitoring and calibration informationhere
background: Replacing AVHRR/3 on MetOp A/B/C

short_description: 5 VIS/MWIR/TIR channels [see detailed characteristics below]
background: New development
scanning_technique: Mechanical, 3-axis stabilised satellite, E-W continuous, S-N stepping

full_name: Michelson Interferometer for Passive Atmospheric Sounding
short_description: Michelson interferometer; range 685-2410 cm-1 (4.15-14.6 µm); spectral resolution 0.035 cm-1 unapodised; 49,300 channels/spectrum; NESR: 50 nW cm-2 sr-1 cm @ 685 cm-1, 4.2 nW cm-2 sr-1 cm @ 2410 cm-1
background: New development
scanning_technique: Limb scanning, fore- and side.  75 s for one vertical scan; 80 scans/orbit, 1145 profiles/day

short_description: 36-channel VIS/IR spectro-radiometer [see detailed characteristics below]More informationhere
background: New development

short_description: 5-channel scanning radiometer on Nimbus 1–3; included TIR window, WV, and CO₂ channels; precursor to HIRS
notes: Nimbus-3 replaced 5–30 µm broadband channel with 20–23 µm channel

short_description: 7 channels. Three telescopes, one for each sub-spectral range (VNIR, SWIR and TIR) [see detailed characteristics below]
background: New development

short_description: 10 VIS/NIR/MWIR/TIR channels [see detailed characteristics below]
background: New development
scanning_technique: Mechanical, 3-axis stabilised satellite, E-W continuous, S-N stepping

short_description: 10 VIS/NIR/MWIR/TIR channels [see detailed characteristics below]
background: Adapted from MSU-GS on Electro-L N1/N2/N3 for the Molniya orbit
scanning_technique: Mechanical, 3-axis stabilised satellite, right-left continuous, top-bottom stepping

short_description: 20 channels in the 0.38-14.25 mm range. 4 channels in VIS, SNR ≥ 200; 16 channels in IR, NEΔT = 0.1 @ 300 K (3.5-4.0 and 7.5-12.5 μm) or 0.3 @ 250 K (5.7-7.0 and 13-15 μm)
background: Evolution of MSU-GS on Electro-L N1/N2/N3
scanning_technique: Mechanical, 3-axis stabilised satellite, E-W continuous, S-N stepping

full_name: Multi-channel medium and far IR range radiometer
short_description: 2-channel radiometer, one in MWIR (3.5-4.1 μm) one in TIR (8.1-9.1 μm).
background: Evolution of the MSU-200 radiometer flown on KANOPUS-V1.  PAN replaced by two IR channels.  Supportive of MSS

short_description: 6 VIS/NIR/SWIR/MWIR/TIR channels [see detailed characteristics below]
background: Evolution and merging of Klimat + MR-2000M1 on Meteor-3M

short_description: 19 channels in the 0.4-12.5 μm range
background: Evolution of MSU-MR flown on Meteor-M N1, N2, N2-1, N2-2

short_description: 5-channel VIS/IR radiometer, 4 in VIS/NIR/SWIR, one in TIR [see detailed characteristics below]
background: Similar to MSU-SK flown on the Resurs-O1 series

short_description: 5-channel VIS/IR radiometer, 4 in VIS/NIR/SWIR, one in TIR [see detailed characteristics below]
background: New development

short_description: 6-channel VIS/IR radiometer, 4 in VIS/NIR/SWIR, two in MWIR/TIR [see detailed characteristics below]
background: Improved version of the MSU-SK flown on Resurs-O1 1/2/3

short_description: Multi-spectral Thermal Imager on Russian spacecraft; detailed band specifications not publicly available
5-channel VIS/NIR/MWIR radiometer [see detailed characteristics below]
background: Part of a combined MW imaging radiometer and VIS/IR imager

short_description: 3 VIS/TIR channels [see detailed characteristics below]Monitoring and calibration informationhere
background: Flown on Meteosat First Generation

short_description: 10 VIS/NIR/SWIR/TIR channels [see detailed characteristics below]Calibration monitoringhere
background: Flown on the FY-1 series

short_description: 6-channel radiometer in the TIR range [see detailed characteristics below]
background: Complementary to MX-VNIR
scanning_technique: Mechanical, addressable everywhere in the disc with variable area coverage

short_description: 3-channel IR radiometer [see detailed characteristics below]
background: New development

short_description: 12-channel radiometer, 8 narrow-bandwidth in the range 0.40-0.89 ?m for ocean colour and vegetation, 4 in the range 3.5-12.7 ?m [see detailed characteristcs below]
background: New development
scanning_technique: Wiskbroom, 1400 km swath, capability of ± 20° off-pointing along track to avoid sunglint

short_description: 3-channel VIS/IR radiometer, including a day/night VIS channel implemented by a photomultiplier [see detailed characteristics below]
background: Flown on all satellites of the DMSP series

short_description: Composed of two similar radiometric channels with pressurised CO2, one for the altitude range 40-60 km, the other for the range 60-90.  The height of the weighting function is changed by modulating the CO2 pressure in the cells, and exploiting the Doppler shift by tilting the telescope along-track
background: Follow-on of SCR flown on Nimbus-5
scanning_technique: Nadir pointing, with along-track tilting capability in the range from -15 to +15 degrees

thermal_bands.purpose: Stratospheric/mesospheric temperature profiling via CO₂ 15 µm absorption
PREFIRE FIR benchmark — lower MWIR/FIR boundary
Arctic/Antarctic FIR outgoing longwave radiation; 12 channels across LWIR window

short_description: NASA CubeSat pair (launched 2024); 64-channel spectrometer 4–53 µm; first systematic FIR measurements of polar regions; MWIR/LWIR range 4–15 µm
notes: Primary science target is far-IR (>15 µm); channels 1-13 (4-15 µm) overlap with MWIR/LWIR TIR range used here

full_name: Stretched Visible and Infrared Spin Scan Radiometer
short_description: 5 VIS/MWIR/TIR channels [see detailed characteristics below]
background: Follow-on of S-VISSR flown on FY-2C/D/E, with narrower VIS bandwidth and improved radiometric performance

full_name: Sounding of the Atmosphere using Broadband Emission Radiometry
short_description: Radiometer with 10 channels spread in the interval 1.27 to 17 μm
background: New development
scanning_technique: Limb sounding. Along track sampling at 400 km intervals

short_description: Pressure-modulated correlation spectrometer with 9 cells for: temperature and CO2 (4.3 and 15 μm), CO (4.7 μm), CH4 and N2O (7.7 μm), NO (5.3 μm) and H2O (2.7 μm and 25-100 μm).  Spectral resolution about 5 cm-1
background: Follow-on of SCR flown on Nimbus-4/5 and PMR flown on Nimbus-6.  Now the scanning is limb instead of nadir
scanning_technique: Limb scanning, both vertically (5-km steps) and in azimuth (100 km)

short_description: MWIR+LWIR microsatellite constellation payload (India) for high-revisit thermal Earth observation
notes: Indian commercial TIR constellation. Limited public technical specifications available as of 2025.

full_name: Surface Biology and Geology — Thermal Infrared (OTTER)
short_description: NASA/ASI joint mission: 8-band TIR at 60 m and 3-day revisit for surface biology and geology
notes: Instrument name OTTER. Joint NASA–ASI (Italy) mission. Coordinates with ESA LSTM and CNES/ISRO TRISHNA.

short_description: 4 channels, two broad-band, two narrow-band [see detailed characteristics below]
background: Developed in the French Laboratoire de Météorologie Dynamique

short_description: 3-channel radiometer, 0.8-1.1 µm, 8.3-9.3 µm and 10.2-11.2 µm
background: As a follow-on of SCMR, a dedicated Heat Capacity Mapping Mission (HCMM) was flown in 1978

short_description: Correlation spectrometer exploiting pressurised CO2 cells, modulated so as to generate 6 channels in the 15 µm spectral band
background: New development

short_description: 16-channel radiometer, with 4 channel implemented by a correlation spectrometer exploiting pressurised CO2 cells, the other 12 by a filter wedge spectrometer [see detailed characteristics below]
background: Follow-on of SCR and FWS flown on Nimbus-4
scanning_technique: Nadir only.  IFOV of the 4 channels around 668.2 cm-1: 40 km; for all other 12 channels: 30 km

short_description: 12 channels (11 narrow-bandwidth, 1 high-resolution broad-bandwidth VIS) [see detailed characteristics below]. For full details of SEVIRI see https://vuser.eumetsat.int/resources/service-statuses/MSG-instruments-status-and-calibration#ID-SEVIRI.Monitoring and calibration informationhere
background: New development

short_description: 19-channel radiometer, 17 narrow-bandwidth in VIS/NIR/SWIR and two broadband in the Thermal IR.  Two channels with three polarisations [see detailed characteristics below]
background: Evolution of GLI flown on ADEOS-2 (2002-2003)

short_description: Nadir viewing infrared interferometer developed by the GDR. Spectral region covered 6.25-25 microns wavelength with a resolution of 5cm-1 after apodisation. An on board black body and deep space view were used for calibration. More details: https://tinyurl.com/2tahhvz3
background: New development - Provided by the German Democratic Republic

short_description: Grating spectrometer utilised to select 8 spectral channels, 0.1 µm wide, centred on 11.12, 13.33, 14.01, 14.16, 14.31, 14.45, 14.76 and 14.95 µm
background: Prototype of future operational IR sounders

short_description: Grating spectrometer utilised to select 14 channels in the spectral range 11.1-35.7 µm [see detailed characteristics below]
background: Follow-on of SIRS-A flown on Nimbus-3.  Scanning capability added

full_name: Sea and Land Surface Temperature Radiometer
short_description: 11-channels with dual viewing directions for accurate atmospheric corrections. Channels 3.74 and 10.85 µm are duplicated for fire detection [see detailed characteristics below]Monitoring and calibration informationhere
background: Evolution of ATSR-2 on ERS-2 and AATSR on Envisat

short_description: 9-channel IR radiometer operating in the range 9.65-18.70 µm
background: New development

short_description: 9-channel IR radiometer operating in the range 9.65-18.70 µm
background: Follow-on of SM flown on the Meteor-2 series

short_description: 19 channels (including one VIS) [see detailed characteristics below]
background: Follow-on of VAS flown on GOES 4 to 7, sepating the imaging from the sounding function

short_description: 19 channels (including one VIS) [see detailed characteristics below]
background: New development

short_description: Two-channel VIR/IR radiometer, 0.52-0.72 ?m and 10.5-12.5 ?m
background: Developed after HRIR flown on Nimbus 1/2/3

full_name: Special Sensor H (also known as Multichannel Filter Radiometer)
short_description: An infrared radiometer with 16 channels from 354 to 1020 cm-1 (28.2um to 9.8um) covering the Mid-IR to Far-IR. See table below for channel list. There is no official documentation on the channel central frequencies in the table  so they should be treated with caution.
background: A new research instrument.

thermal_bands.purpose: Atmospheric sounding / cloud detection (16 channels 354–1020 cm⁻¹)

short_description: 2-channel TIR radiometer around 11 and 12 μm [see details below]
background: In connection with ocean colour from OCM

short_description: Pressure-modulated spectrometer, operating around the wavelengths 14.92, 14.93 and 14.94 µm
background: Follow-on of SCR flown on Nimbus-4/5 and PMR flown on Nimbus-6.  Part of the TIROS Operational Vertical Sounder (TOVS) package

short_description: 2-channel radiometer, VIS (0.46-0.7 µm) and TIR (10.5-12.5 µm)
background: New development
scanning_technique: Mechanical scanning, E-W and N-S (3-axis stabilised platform)

short_description: High-resolution uncooled LWIR satellite: 3.3 m resolution using 120-cm deployable telescope
notes: HIBISCUS instrument on NanoAvionics MP42 bus. Uncooled microbolometer. 940,000 km²/hour data collection. Launch H2 2026.

full_name: Thermal And Near infrared Sensor for carbon Observation — 3
short_description: Advanced FTS on GOSAT-GW with TIR+SWIR capability for greenhouse gas profiling from 3.6–15.4 µm
notes: GOSAT-GW launched 2024. TANSO-3 is an improved FTS versus TANSO-FTS/2 on GOSAT-2.

full_name: Thermal And Near infrared Sensor for carbon Observations - Fourier Transform Spectrometer
short_description: 4–band interferometer (3 in NIR/SWIR, 1 in TIR) [see detailed characteristics below]
background: New development
primary_objectives: Height of the top of PBL

full_name: Thermal And Near infrared Sensor for carbon Observations - Fourier Transform Spectrometer / 2
short_description: 5–band interferometer (3 in NIR/SWIR, 2 in TIR).  Polarisation observation in the 3 short-wave bands [see detailed characteristics below]
background: Evolution of TANSO-FTS.  Split TIR range
primary_objectives: Height of the top of PBL

short_description: Imaging interferometer, four bands, 162162 channels [see detailed characteristics below]More informationhere
background: New development
scanning_technique: Each detector subtends a 2.3 km IFOV at the lowest altitude (surface); the 16 detectors simultaneously observe a range of altitudes of 37 km; the 10 transverse steps cover 23 km (horizontal). The limb modes is alternative to the cross-nadir mode

short_description: Imaging interferometer, four bands, 43,750 channels [see detailed characteristics below]More informationhere
background: New development

short_description: Two-channel IR radiometer, 6.5-7.0 ?m and 10.5-12.5 ?m.  Direct read-out on Nimbus-4
background: Prototype of multichannel IR radiometers

short_description: Three channel IR radiometer with a spatial resolution of 30m. The wavelength of the channels are 8-10.5 um, 10.3-11.3 um and 11.5-12.5 um.
background: A new development.

short_description: 2 TIR channels [see detailed characteristic below]
background: New development on Landsat.8.  Extended design lifetime on Landsat-9 (TIRS-2)

short_description: 7 channels (6 in VIS/NIR/SWIR, one in TIR) [see detailed characteristics below]
background: New development
scanning_technique: Wiskbroom; 6000 pixel/line (6 short-wave channels), 1500 pixel/line (TIR); swath 185 km
notes: TIR Band 6 at 120m; VIS/NIR at 30m. Landsat-5 operated 29 years, longest in series.

short_description: Information only partially available.  4 TIR channels in the range 8.6-11.5 μm, 6 VNIR channels in the range 485-2130 nm.
background: New development
notes: Joint CNES (France) / ISRO (India) mission. 57 m land/coast; 1 km ocean. Also carries VIS/NIR/SWIR payload.

short_description: Filter radiometer alternatively used for cloud imagery (2 channels: 0.55-0.75 μm and 10.5-12.6 μm) or atmospheric sounding (12 channels centred on 3.94, 4.44, 4.51, 6.7, 7.2, 11.2, 12.7, 13.3, 14.0, 14.2, 14.5 and 14.7 μm).  The required SNR is achieved by selecting appropriate spatial resolution and number of repeated scan lines for the different channels
background: Implemented by adding absorbing channels to VISSR flown on SMS 1/2 and GOES 1/2/3

short_description: 2-channel VIS/IR radiometer, 0.55-0.75 and 10.5-12.5 µm
background: New development
scanning_technique: Mechanical, 3-axis stabilised satellite, E-W continuous, S-N stepping

short_description: 3 VIS/TIR channels [see detailed characteristics below]
background: Heritage of VHRR flown on ATS-6

short_description: 22 channels, including a day/night 0.7 µm channel [see detailed characteristics below]. More details here: https://www.star.nesdis.noaa.gov/jpss/VIIRS.php
background: New development, developed in the NPOESS framework

full_name: Visible Infrared Imaging Radiometer Suite — JPSS-3
short_description: Identical VIIRS copy on JPSS-3 (NOAA-22); 22 bands including 7 TIR from 3.7–12.0 µm at 750 m
notes: Identical to VIIRS on Suomi-NPP, NOAA-20, NOAA-21. JPSS-3 launch ~2027.

short_description: ASTER-like 4-band LWIR sensor on Gaofen-5 series; 2 km resolution; land surface temperature and emissivity
notes: Band specifications are provisional; OSCAR lists as 'similar to ASTER'. Verify against CNSA instrument documentation.

short_description: Two-channel VIS (0.49-0.94 µm) and IR (10.5-12.5 µm) radiometer.  IR NE?T: 4 K @ 185 K or 1 K @ 300 K
background: Adaptation of SR flown on NOAA 1 to 5

short_description: 5 VIS/IR channels [see detailed characteristics below]
background: Adapted from AVHRR/3 (NIR channel missing)

short_description: 4-channel radiometer, 1 VIS, 3 TIR including a water-vapour channel [see detailed characteristics below]
